operations.rationale: Replace the plan-node lifecycle-state table with reader prose while preserving the canonical AI/research state matrix.
content_lines: Plan nodes move through lifecycle states so a useful idea does not become execution authority by accident. Proposed nodes are decomposition candidates only. Nodes blocked on context need missing material or a narrower scope. Nodes blocked on authority need a new contract, approval route, or refusal. Nodes blocked on dependency wait for upstream work, and nodes blocked on verification need a success predicate before they can be dispatched.

A dispatchable node is different from a plausible node: its constraints, context, authority, dependencies, and verification plan are all satisfied. Only then may it lower into a typed job. Once dispatched, it must preserve job parentage so later audit can trace the action back to the accepted command and plan node.

Replanned nodes record what changed after feedback while keeping the original authority and stop-condition boundaries visible. Stopped nodes preserve the reason work ended, whether denial, budget exhaustion, risk, or a triggered stop condition. These states let the plan remain useful before execution and accountable after execution.
The reader-level point is simple: the graph can contain many kinds of work, but only a narrow subset is authorized work.
